subject: Geography
title: Smart Cities
description: A quick win: understand Smart Cities.

hook.text: Songdo, South Korea, was built from scratch as a 'smart city' with sensors in every system.

buildup.text: Trash is vacuumed through underground tubes. Traffic lights adjust to real-time flow.

discovery.text: Sensors monitor air quality, energy use, and water pressure across the entire city.

twist.text: Critics call it soulless—a city designed by engineers, not communities.

climax.text: Smart technology can optimize a city, but it can't create the culture that makes one lovable.

punchline.text: A perfect city on paper isn't always perfect to live in.

quiz.question: What criticism do smart cities like Songdo face?
options: They feel soulless, designed by engineers not communities
They are too expensive to maintain
